pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 17 creates file with 11 female first names: Isabelle, Martine, Catherine, Nathalie, Sandrine, Aurélie, Chantal, Monique, Marie, Julie, Sophie
setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py all reference database 'DrTuxTest'

setup_task.sh lines 58-61 insert 4 target cases: GUID_T1 (Isabelle), GUID_T2 (Martine), GUID_T3 (Catherine), GUID_T4 (Marie) - all female names misclassified as 'H' (male). export_result.sh line 25 confirms these GUIDs.

setup_task.sh lines 64-66 insert 3 distractor cases: GUID_D1 (Jean-Marie - compound name with Marie but male), GUID_D2 (Jean-Pierre), GUID_D3 (Claude - ambiguous). export_result.sh line 26 confirms these GUIDs.

setup_task.sh lines 69-70 insert 2 baseline cases: GUID_B1 (Pierre - male, correctly classified as 'H') and GUID_B2 (Julie - female, correctly classified as 'F'). export_result.sh line 27 defines baseline_guids = ['GUID_B1', 'GUID_B2'], contradicting task.json metadata which claims only ['GUID_B1'].

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a synthetic test database with 7 test patients. Target cases (4): GUID_T1 (Isabelle), GUID_T2 (Martine), GUID_T3 (Catherine), GUID_T4 (Marie) - all female-named patients incorrectly classified as male that must be corrected to female. Distractor cases (3): GUID_D1 (Jean-Marie), GUID_D2 (Jean-Pierre), GUID_D3 (Claude) - compound or ambiguous names that must remain male. Baseline cases include at least GUID_B1 (Pierre, male) and GUID_B2 (Julie, female) that should remain unchanged. Reference file contains 11 female first names including Marie and Julie. Critical constraint: strict name matching required (Jean-Marie must remain male even though Marie is in the list).
